channel.title: Home on Jitachi
channel.description: Recent content in Home on Jitachi

item.title: Railway → México City
item.description:
At Railway, we have the tradition of making a location-specific artwork for every trip. In the past, we have visited Hawaii, Dubai, Bali, New Orleans, New Buffalo, La Palma (Spain), Lisbon, and for this last edition, we visited Mexico City.

The artwork formula is simple, picture the Railway train arriving to the retreat location. Ideally, use a recognizable monument and shove a train.

For Mexico City, I chose the Angel de la Independencia as the center piece of the artwork. Not all places have a relevant piece of architecture, some of them are just normal buildings.

item.title: Spacing and Density
item.description:
Spacing is used in visual design to create visual relationships and hierarchy. Think of space as the “empty” space you leave between one note and the next one to form rhythm.

Have you noticed how the horizontal space between characters (tracking) is smaller than the blank space we put between “groups” of letter we call words, and how that space is smaller than the vertical space between vertical lines? So now we have lines.

item.title: Poop pods
Railway Metal
item.description: For the release of our Metal regions, I decided to make a conmemorative tshirt, of course featuring a fake trash metal logo

item.title: testy test
item.description:
Performance Upgrades

It is for these reasons that I regard the decision last year to shift our efforts in space from low to high gear as among the most important decisions that will be made during my incumbency in the office of the Presidency.

  • APR Engine (ECU) Tune Stage 1
  • APR Transmission (TCU) Tune Stage 1
  • NGK Performance Spark Plugs
  • Racingline R600 Closed Intake
  • Racingline Turbo inlet pipe & hoses
  • APR Turbo muffler delete
  • Integrated Engineering Performance Intercooler

item.title: testy test
item.description: Senior Product Designer ft Software QA

item.title: Styleguide
item.description: Local styles for a happier development

item.title: About
item.description:
Hello, I'm Jitachi

I like to study the relationship we have with the artifacts we create for ourselves, how they shape the way we think, and how they expand what we believe is possible.

In simple terms, I’m a designer. I’ve been obsessed with technology, magic, and modding my surroundings since I was a kid. Creating artifacts to help others move forward is my love language.
